content.problemStatement:
Suppose Chef is stuck on an island and currently he has xxx units of food supply and yyy units of water supply in total that he could collect from the island. He needs xrx_rxr‚Äã units of food supply and yry_ryr‚Äã units of water supply per day at the minimal to have sufficient energy to build a boat from the woods and also to live for another day. Assuming it takes exactly DDD days to build the boat and reach the shore, tell whether Chef has the sufficient amount of supplies to be able to reach the shore by building the boat?

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, five integers x,y,xr,yr,Dx, y, x_r, y_r, Dx,y,xr‚Äã,yr‚Äã,D.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line answer "YES" if Chef can reach the shore by building the boat and "NO" if not (without quotes).

You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§3001 \leq T \leq 3001‚â§T‚â§300
1‚â§x,y‚â§1001 \leq x, y \leq 1001‚â§x,y‚â§100
1‚â§xr,yr,D‚â§101 \leq x_r, y_r, D \leq 101‚â§xr‚Äã,yr‚Äã,D‚â§10
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains five space‚Äëseparated integers: x y xr yr D, where x and y are the total units of food and water Chef has, xr and yr are the required units of food and water per day, and D is the number of days needed to build the boat and reach the shore.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef can survive for at least D days with the given supplies, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: food lasts 4 days (4/1) and water lasts 2 days (2/1); min=2 >= D=1 ‚Üí YES.\nTest case 2: food lasts 4 days, water lasts floor(2/3)=0 days; min=0 < D=1 ‚Üí NO.\nTest case 3: food lasts floor(4/4)=1 day, water lasts floor(2/2)=1 day; min=1 < D=2 ‚Üí NO.

solution.approach: For each test case compute how many whole days the food can last: foodDays = x // xr, and how many whole days the water can last: waterDays = y // yr. The number of days Chef can survive is the minimum of these two values because both resources are needed each day. If this minimum is at least D, print YES, otherwise NO. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) ‚Äì constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) ‚Äì only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        x = int(next(it)); y = int(next(it)); xr = int(next(it)); yr = int(next(it)); D = int(next(it))
        food_days = x // xr
        water_days = y // yr
        survive = food_days if food_days < water_days else water_days
        out.append('YES' if survive >= D else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
